title: Israel and Iran Have Divided Democrats and Republicans. Will They Ever Be the Same?
summary: Raging internal debates over foreign policy threaten both parties’ fortunes in November — and in 2028. Is a major ideological shift underway?

title: China Emerges as a Relative Winner From Strait of Hormuz Crisis
summary: A report examines how the war in Iran has weighed on Asian economies, but left China in a more advantageous position.

title: Iran Risks Peace Talks With U.S. to Maintain Leverage Over Strait
summary: Iran sees its control over the Strait of Hormuz as critical leverage in peace talks with the United States. It seems willing to risk the cease-fire to maintain that power.

title: Oil Prices Rise on Possible Deal to Suspend Attacks in Gulf
summary: Oil prices edged higher after an American official said the U.S. and Iran had agreed to halt attacks in a key shipping lane, raising hopes that hostilities would not escalate.

title: With Final Decisions Ahead, the Supreme Court Is Sharply Divided
summary: The justices will decide this week whether President Trump can end the guarantee of birthright citizenship and fire a leader of the independent Federal Reserve.

title: Deep Under the Rubble, Rescuers Find an 11-Year-Old Boy Alive
summary: A Colombian rescue team worked for six hours to recover the child, Moises, from under nearly 10 feet of rubble in La Guaira. His rescue was captured on video.

title: Two Earthquakes Hit Venezuela: What to Know About Death Toll, Damage and Rescue Response
summary: Back-to-back quakes in northern Venezuela killed more than 1,400 people and set off an agonizing search for survivors. It was the latest ordeal for a country rocked by economic and political turmoil.

title: In Venezuela, a Community Comes Together to Search for Earthquake Survivors
summary: Volunteers in a middle-class neighborhood in Caracas used drills, picks and hammers to break through concrete, trying to find anyone in need of rescue.

title: The Best Movies of 2026 So Far, and Where to Watch Them
summary: Now is a good time to catch up on films you may have missed. Our critics have thoughts.

title: ‘Supergirl’ Falters at the Box Office, Testing DC Studios’ Reboot
summary: Overall, ticket sales for the weekend were up 21 percent from last year. But the expensive “Supergirl” debuted as a disappointing second, with an estimated $38 million.

title: How Warsh Has Begun to Change the Fed
summary: Just weeks into his job as chairman of the Federal Reserve, Kevin M. Warsh has embraced some of the central bank’s conventions while signaling seismic shifts ahead.

title: Whatever You Do in Russia, Don’t Talk About the War
summary: As Ukraine brings the war home to Russia, officials hesitate to designate shelters and blast sirens, downplaying the conflict’s consequences with euphemisms.

title: Could GLP-1 Drugs Help You Live Longer?
summary: Experts are intrigued by the theory. Here’s what early research suggests.

title: Pakistan Attacks Afghanistan, Killing More Than 30
summary: The Taliban said that airstrikes left 36 civilians dead, while Pakistan claimed that its assault killed 32 militants, in the latest in a monthslong conflict.

title: Liberals Are Preaching Moral Values. What Took Them So Long?
summary: Until recently, the language of morality belonged to social conservatives. But the history of liberalism suggests that values have been at its core all along.

title: For These Teens in Mexico, Soccer Is Life. Now, the Cartels Want In.
summary: In Celaya, Mexico, local cartels have turned soccer fields into bloody battlefields as they have co-opted a local league for teenagers as their latest business endeavor.

title: The Trump Vibe Shift Was Just a Mirage
summary: The country does not love its president.

title: Parents Can’t Give Their Kids Everything. Siblings Can Help.
summary: There are virtues in growing up in a pack.

title: A ‘Heat Dome’ Could Bring Triple- Digit Temperatures to the Midwest and Eastern U.S.
summary: Forecasters warned that temperatures could break records in some areas. The heat is expected to last into the Fourth of July holiday weekend.
